verdict: data_limited. Vela-2024 is the FIRST real, public, PHASE-CONNECTED wide-ln(tau) Vela recovery (Delta nu/nu=2.38e-06, tau_d={15, 2.4, 0.39} d) -- a clear step up from PG.06b's per-obs H-test, and the widest real recovery reached so far (2.55 comb periods vs J0537's ~1.9). The detector is injection-validated at the real Vela cadence (cascade comb found p=0.002, smooth power law rejected p=0.18); reusing PG.06's range-power curve, a strong comb at this reach is localised 95% of the time (vs 0% at J0537's ~1.9 periods). So RANGE is NO LONGER the dominant wall. Yet omega=2.583 is NOT special in the recovery (p=0.23; shuffle p=0.60; not the smallest-p member of the off-kernel lambda battery), and the ln-tau-stacked 2016/2019/2021/2024 recoveries (reach 3.41 periods) are equally flat (p=0.53). This is data_limited, NOT a well-powered kill, for two honest reasons: (1) the public product is the SMOOTH parametric glitch MODEL (permanent jump + 2-3 exponentials), not the residual nu(t) -- a ~2% (eps~exp(-pi^2/ln lambda)) comb, if present, lives in the RESIDUALS to that fit, which the small release does not contain, so any null here is null-by-construction; (2) at the predicted ~2% amplitude a single recovery is amplitude-limited, and only ~4 Vela giant glitches exist (stacking buys amplitude ~sqrt(N)~2x, not range -- PG.06). Decisive next step: the phase-connected IAR/MPRO ToA RESIDUALS over a full inter-glitch baseline (2021->2024 ~1012 d ~2.8 periods), glitch model removed, superposed-epoch stacked. No claim; a hit would be a universal-DSI coincidence in the NS interior, not TFPT confirmation. Firewall intact.
